reasoning: The cutoff date value is the weekly observation for Jan 30, which is 3.50%. The daily yield on the Friday of that week (Jan 30) closed at 3.48%, below the weekly average. Because the next week begins with an interest rate of 3.48%, it faces an immediate headwind to average above 3.50%. Furthermore, the 1-year yield is pricing in rate cuts later in the year, which mechanically creates a slight downward drift in yields as the window rolls forward. Given the mathematical disadvantage of starting from 3.48%, the probability of the Feb 6 weekly average exceeding 3.50% is around 32%. For the March 3 resolution (Feb 27 observation), there is a longer time horizon allowing for more random walk variance, moving the probability closer to 50%, though the structural downward drift caused by expectations of H2 Fed rate cuts keeps it slightly below 50% at around 42%.
